Sun Pharma beams up Organon in $11.75B acquisition
snippet: Sun Pharmaceutical is bringing Merck spinoff Organon into the fold, paying $11.75 billion in hopes of becoming a top global biosimilar player.

title: Lilly lands third oncology deal in 3 weeks, inking up to $2.3B for Ajax
snippet: Eli Lilly is picking up Ajax Therapeutics and its once-daily oral blood cancer candidate. The deal follows the pharma’s buyouts of ADC specialist CrossBridge Bio and in vivo CAR T company Kelonia Therapeutics.

title: Intellia heads to FDA after notching first late-stage win for in vivo gene editor
snippet: With Phase 3 data in hand, Intellia Therapeutics is seeking approval for its in vivo CRISPR gene editing therapy for hereditary angioedema.

title: UCSF, Par Health hit with FDA warning letters for environmental monitoring issues
snippet: FDA inspectors warned UCSF radiopharmaceutical facility that losing environmental control could pose a serious hazard to patients.

title: Rejuvenate crowdfunds development of gene therapy for aging, chronic diseases
snippet: George Church’s Rejuvenate Bio is turning to social networks to help fund its work on one-time gene therapies targeting chronic diseases and root causes of aging.

title: J&J puts blockbuster blood thinner Xarelto on TrumpRx
snippet: After striking a Most Favored Nation deal with the White House in January, Johnson & Johnson will now offer Xarelto at 68% off on TrumpRx, dropping its price from $611.82 to $197 per 30-pill pack.

title: Gene therapy leaders aim to help more Baby KJs with novel regulatory models
snippet: As cell and gene therapy leaders gathered in Maryland to discuss accelerating clinical trials in children, one “cutting edge” session focused on the need to expedite more bespoke gene editing treatments like the one that saved young KJ Muldoon.

title: Daiichi Sankyo shares slip after delaying annual earnings report
snippet: Daiichi Sankyo’s full-year report was originally scheduled for April 27 but has now been pushed back to May 11. That same day, the pharma expects to release its five-year business plan.

title: Pfizer culls early PD-L1 asset after series of clinical wins, deals in cancer
snippet: Pfizer’s decision to cut its early-stage cancer asset was due to “strategic business reasons” and not driven by safety or efficacy concerns.

title: Avalyn seeks public debut to support new lung drug formulations
snippet: Analysts are cautiously optimistic about an IPO rebound for biopharma. BioSpace is keeping track of companies that seek to trade on the public markets this year.

title: Regeneron finally joins Trump’s drug pricing push while notching landmark approval
snippet: Of the 17 companies that were implored by the White House last July to apply Most Favored Nation pricing to their drugs, Regeneron is the last to agree—the same day the FDA greenlit its gene therapy for hearing loss in kids.

title: Opinion: Prasad’s FDA exit good for rare diseases but new CBER head must repair eroded trust

title: FDA approval of Regeneron’s hearing loss gene therapy breaks barriers
snippet: Approved Thursday via the FDA's Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher program, Otarmeni is the first gene therapy for hearing loss—and the first treatment to target an underlying cause of the condition.

journalName: Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association
snippet: The COVID-19 pandemic underscored the impact of severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) infection on worsening the severity of Alzheimer's disease (AD) neuropathology and disease progression. Aged 5xFAD and wild-type (WT) mice were infected with a mouse-adapted SARS-CoV-2 (MA10), and extensive characterization of molecular and cellular....

title: Clinicopathologic Characterization of Muscle Biopsy Findings in Overlap Myositis: A Single Tertiary Care Center Experience.
snippet: This study aims to describe the muscle biopsy features of overlap myositis (OM) and compare them with other inflammatory myopathies. We analyzed clinical, serological findings, and histopathological patterns in muscle biopsies to understand the features of OM. Muscle biopsies submitted from patients diagnosed with OM between January 2023 and October 2024 at...

title: Multimodal Radiogenomic Imaging in Oropharyngeal Squamous Cell Carcinoma: Implications for Dentomaxillofacial Radiology.
snippet: Radiogenomics examines associations between imaging phenotypes and underlying biological characteristics across cancer types. This structured narrative review focuses on oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (OPSCC) and evaluates how genomic programs characteristic of HPV-positive and HPV-negative tumors have been investigated across computed tomography (CT...

title: FUNCellA: A Tool for Single-Sample Enrichment Analysis and Relative Pathway Activity Estimation in Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Data.
snippet: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-Seq) enables advanced analysis of cellular heterogeneity; however, due to data sparsity and noise, analyzing single genes can be challenging. While pathway activity scoring offers a robust alternative to standard single-gene analysis, most available enrichment methods were developed for bulk RNA-Seq or microarray data, so t...

title: Interaction networks of macrophage glycolysis and inflammation in sepsis: mechanisms and therapeutic potential.
snippet: Sepsis is a severe threat to human health with high mortality rates, but so far its pathogenesis is unclear and lacks effective therapeutic drugs. Macrophages function as one of the most important innate immune cells and play an integral role in the sepsis inflammatory process. Recently, studies have shown that its immune function is associated with the War...

title: Dual Mechanisms of Naru Sanwei Pills in Gout: NLRP3 Inflammasome Inhibition and Uric Acid Regulation.
snippet: Gout is a metabolic inflammatory disorder driven by monosodium urate (MSU) crystal-induced activation of the NLRP3 inflammasome and frequently accompanied by hyperuricemia-related renal injury. This study aimed to investigate the therapeutic effects of Naru Sanwei Pills (Naru-3) on gout and to elucidate the underlying anti-inflammatory and uric acid-lowerin...
